In step two, you're going to be submitting articles. I recommend you use an article submitter program that's actually called article submitter. This one's created by Brad Callan. I believe it to be one of the best in the marketplace. He has a free one that you can use. It doesn't have all the bells and whistles unlocked, but it is quite usable in the free version. I've tested a lot of these out, and I do believe that this one is the best one. It's the one that I'm using personally after buying three or four of these. So anyways, I'm using the platinum version, but you can use the free version. The free version will let you send out to 85 article directories. The platinum version will just let you send out to like 250. The free version works just fine. First thing you're going to need to do is you're going to need to go and register accounts at every single one of the article directories. So for instance, this machine will let you do is I can double click on one of these article directories, and then it will take me there, and it will automatically submit my login details here. So you should have done it. Let me try again. Some of them it takes two or three tries. Let me try another one that's usually pretty good for us. Okay, so this is one that we already have an account for. I realized now where I was messing up. The ones that I already have an account for, it will automatically put my login information here. But if I don't have an account for it, then what I'm going to do is I'm going to double click on the directory. It's going to take me to where I need to click the sign up button. Click sign up. It fills out all my details, and then I just click submit, and it goes and registers an account for me. Very easy to do. You go through and just register to all of them out there, and then you're going to put your login information here. So you type in your username, and you type in your password for the account that you just registered. Alright, now going back to the main screen here. The next thing you're going to want to do is you're going to want to fill out all that information that you registered to all the article directories. It should all be the same information. I've got some samples here. Let me make this a little bit bigger so you guys can see it. Alright, so you're going to need a username and password, first name, last name, email address, city, state, zip code. You're going to use all that information to register all your accounts. You can pay for somebody to register your accounts for you on a site like Script Lance. It usually costs you $20 or $30, and they'll go and take the three or four hours that it takes to go and submit all your details to all the different accounts and register accounts for you. Or you can do it yourself. So you use all that information, and you're going to type it here into this account information box. I can't open it up because it'll show you private information. It'll show you my passwords. So I'm not going to open that button, but when you open it, you're going to be entering that same information that I just showed you. Now you've got all your accounts squared away. You've got all your information squared away. And then what you're going to do is you're going to need to add an article. So I'm just going to click on Edit Articles so that you can see all the different steps that I took. And once you have a bunch of articles, they'll all be loaded in here. My Outsourcer runs this program for me, so I don't have a bunch of them listed in mine, but you would have a bunch of them listed down here. So click Edit Article. You're going to need to put your article title. And remember, in the lesson, I told you to have your keyword in your article title. My keyword was Flip Websites. I have it in here twice. You're going to need to pick a category. Business is as close to flipping websites as I can get. I don't want to type in flipping websites because that's too narrow, and not every article directory will have that section. But they will all have a business section. Then you're going to put your author first name and last name. You can put a pen name here, a client name here, a mascot name, whatever you want. You can put your first and last name there. That's where it's going to say, you know, this article was written by and then whoever. And then you're going to put your email. Next thing, you're going to need to put the article resources. And this is that resource box that I told you about where you're going to have a little brag session about you as well as a link. And that's how people are going to read your article. If they like your article, they're going to read your resource box and then want to follow back and get more information about you and what you offer. So you're going to put the resource box here and then you're going to put the HTML version of the resource box, which just means that this one is the one that has a link. And you can see that I have the link right here. Ahref goes to my site flipping website. And the keyword is flip websites. I've got my keywords here, which is related keywords or tags. I've got a little one sentence description. I don't use the HTML description here. And then you're just going to paste your article body in here. If you're using Microsoft Word to write your articles, make sure you paste them into a document like notepad. Or if you're on a Mac, it might be BBedited or BB Express. But you want to dump your article into a program that's not going to attach a bunch of code to it behind the scenes like Microsoft Word does. It'll automatically add all this formatting code and you want to strip it away from that. So that's why I drop it into notepad or some other basic text program. And then I copy it into here. So there's my article and then I need to make an article HTML body. Now what this section does is it flattens all my paragraphs to make it look like one long sentence. And then I need to make sure, because it's HTML, I need to make sure I tell the article directory what is a paragraph. So I put a beginning p tag at the beginning of every paragraph and then I put an ending p tag at the end of every paragraph. And you can tell the difference between the beginning and the end back here because the ending one has that little forward slash and there a backslash. That means it's an ending p tag and that's the HTML code for paragraph. So that tells it that everything is okay. Each of these lines is a separate paragraph and to break it up with spaces in between so it doesn't look like one big giant block of text. Alright, and then when you're done, you're just going to click OK. And then what you're going to do is you're going to scroll down to one of the article directories. I'm going to go to one that I have an account with so we can get through this easily. It's going to automatically log in and I click submit. It automatically takes me to the article submission form. It automatically posts all the information about my article to all the different forms. And then all I'm going to do is I'm going to click the submit button. I've already submitted this article so I'm not going to submit it twice because that could get me in trouble. They don't like having the same article in their multiple times because that looks kind of spamming. But that's as easy as it is. I just enter my account information, I enter my article information, and then I go to the site and I hit submit and I just do that over and over again until all of them are finished. And again, you can hire an outsourcer to do something like this for you. I pay my outsource for $3.50 an hour. They work in the Philippines so $3.50 an hour is very good pay for them. It's a high paying job for them. And I get a great task done for me and they get a good paycheck and everybody's happy. And that's how you submit your articles.
